beats.activity: Wake up, shower, and morning tea at home while listening to rain on the windows.
beats.reasoning: Tuesday morning after energizing Monday; rain outside means a slower, introspective start. Maren wakes naturally and settles into the day rather than rushing.

beats.activity: Commute to Tukwila via I-5, avoiding SR-520 closure by heading south through Seattle.
beats.reasoning: SR-520 eastbound is closed 9am–noon; Maren leaves early enough to avoid the closure and take I-5 south instead. Grey drizzle won't deter her commute—she's already in teaching mode.

beats.activity: Teach morning yoga class and prep studio for next session.; Teach second yoga class of the day; rain continues outside.; Admin and prep: check messages, review wellness coaching notes, and tidy the studio.; Teach afternoon yoga class and individual coaching session.
beats.reasoning: Tuesdays are usually lighter than Mondays, but Maren has committed classes. She's already here, so she settles in for teaching.

beats.activity: Commute back to Seattle via I-5, avoiding SR-520 closure. Rain is clearing.
beats.reasoning: Heading back north; the bridge closure is still in effect until noon, but Maren times her departure to avoid it. She's heading toward Capitol Hill for an errand.

beats.activity: Grab lunch and pick up yarn at local shop; rain has mostly stopped.
beats.reasoning: Maren takes the scenic route back and stops in Capitol Hill for lunch and knitting supplies. The rain break makes it pleasant to walk around, and it's an easy detour she enjoys.

beats.activity: Commute home to Fremont; afternoon light is soft and grey.
beats.reasoning: Short drive home from Capitol Hill to Fremont. Maren values the quiet time to decompress after teaching and let the day settle.

beats.activity: Settle in at home with new yarn, knit while listening to a podcast, and work on poetry notes for the next open-mic.
beats.reasoning: Post-work wind-down with knitting and creative work. Yesterday's energy was good (40/100 suggests a grounded but not overly energetic mood), so Maren stays home and lets the afternoon unfold at her own pace.

beats.activity: Prepare a simple dinner with fresh herbs from yesterday's Whole Foods run.
beats.reasoning: Maren cooks at home; the greens and herbs she picked up yesterday are fresh and ready. This is part of her evening ritual and self-care practice.

diary:
Woke to that perfect sound of rain on the roof—the kind that makes you want to linger under the covers with tea. But the studio needed me, so I headed south through Seattle to avoid the bridge mess. Three classes back-to-back in Tukwila kept me grounded, literally and figuratively. There's something about moving through those familiar sequences with students that centers me, even on grey days.

Got back to Capitol Hill around lunchtime and ducked into the yarn shop—couldn't resist picking up a deep indigo wool I've been eyeing. The rain was mostly gone by then, just that soft, tired light hanging over everything. Spent the evening knitting on the couch, half-listening to a podcast about urban gardening, scribbling down lines for the open-mic next week. Something about the day's quiet rhythm settled into my chest.
thought_process: The bridge closure could've derailed my whole day, but I made the pivot early—southbound through I-5 felt safer than gambling on the delay. I needed the structure of teaching anyway; those classes anchored me. The rain matched my mood perfectly, actually. I've been a little restless lately, and the weather gave me permission to just be slow and internal. The yarn purchase was practical but also tender—I'm leaning into handmade things again. Poetry's been calling louder than usual. By evening, I felt the day had given me exactly what I needed: movement, students, solitude, and time to create. No rush.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
